aliases: National National Parks ServicePark Service, NPS
National United States Parks ServicePark Service of the United States
description_wiki: The National Park Service (NPS) is an agency of the United States federal government, within the United States Department of the Interior.

description: southernmost part of the island of Manhattan in New York City, United States
description_wiki: Lower Manhattan, also known as Downtown Manhattan, is the southernmost part of the New York City borough of Manhattan.

description: passenger ferry service in New York City, New York, United States
description_wiki: The Staten Island Ferry is a fare-free passenger ferry route operated by the New York City Department of Transportation.

aliases: Staten Island borough, New York City
description: borough of New York City, New York, United States of America
description_wiki: Staten Island  ( STAT-ən) is the southernmost of the five boroughs of New York City, coextensive with Richmond County and situated at the southernmost point of the State of New York.

aliases: The City So Nice They Named It Twice
description: most populous city in the United States
description_wiki: New York, often called New York City (NYC), is the most populous city in the United States.

description: neighborhood in Manhattan, New York City, New York, USA
description_wiki: Upper Manhattan is the northern section of the New York City borough of Manhattan.

description: island in New York Harbor in the United States of America
description_wiki: Ellis Island is an island in New York Harbor, within the U.S. states of New Jersey and New York.

description: city in New Jersey, United States
description_wiki: Jersey City is the second-most populous city in the U.S. state of New Jersey, after Newark.

description: park located on Upper New York Bay in Jersey City, New Jersey
description_wiki: Liberty State Park (LSP) is a 1,212 acres (490 ha) state park in the U.S. state of New Jersey, located on Upper New York Bay in Jersey City opposite Liberty Island and Ellis Island.

description: public park in Manhattan, New York City, New York, United States
former skyscraper complex in Manhattan, New York (1968-2001)
description_wiki: The original World Trade Center (WTC) was a complex of seven buildings in the Financial District of Lower Manhattan in New York City.

aliases: The Statue of Liberty Enlightening the World
description: American icon in New York, United States
description_wiki: The Statue of Liberty (Liberty Enlightening the World; French: La Liberté éclairant le monde) is a colossal neoclassical sculpture of a robed and crowned woman on Liberty Island in New York Harbor, within New York City.

surface_forms: our sister park at Gateway National Recreation Area
description: parks and beaches in New York City and New Jersey
description_wiki: Gateway National Recreation Area is a 26,607-acre (10,767 ha)  U.S. National Recreation Area in New York City and Monmouth County, New Jersey.

Fort Wadsworth is a former United States military installation on Staten Island in New York City, situated on The Narrows, which divide New York Bay into Upper and Lower bays, a natural point for defense of the Upper Bay, Manhattan, and beyond.

description: security and rescue military service branch for U.S. waters
description_wiki: The United States Coast Guard (USCG) is the maritime security, search and rescue, and law enforcement service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

aliases: City of New York Police Department
description: municipal police force in the United States

description_wiki: The United States Park Police (USPP) is the oldest uniformed federal law enforcement agency in the United States.

surface_forms: what had happened in Lower Manhattan
description_wiki: The September 11 attacks, colloquially known as 9/11, were a coordinated series of suicide attacks perpetrated by the Islamist terrorist organization al-Qaeda against the United States in 2001.
